mechanism_narrative: In a Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP) regime where the federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, the compression of discount rates applied to future equity cash flows dominates valuation models, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in ZIRP periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer income, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive payroll changes do not correlate with positive 20-day forward returns (correlation coefficient < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) exhibits a month-over-month increase exceeding 0.4%, markets price in a higher probability of premature monetary tightening, causing equity valuations to contract and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the high inflation signal, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in low-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 1.2 million units annually indicate robust construction activity and consumer confidence in durable goods, which serves as a leading indicator for broad economic expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or high housing start levels fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns (correlation coefficient < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An index value for Industrial Production (INDPRO) above 100 signals that the manufacturing and industrial sectors are operating above their 2017 baseline capacity, reflecting strong supply-side momentum that supports corporate output and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or INDPRO levels above 100 do not correlate with positive 20-day forward returns (correlation coefficient < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail Sales (RSAFS) values exceeding 500,000 million dollars indicate sustained high consumer spending momentum, which is the primary driver of US corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or high retail sales levels fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns (correlation coefficient < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) exceeds 6.0%, it signals significant labor market slack and recession risk, causing investors to de-risk portfolios and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the high unemployment signal, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where GDP (GDPC1) shows recovery or high levels while the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) remains elevated creates a 'jobless recovery' narrative, increasing uncertainty about the sustainability of growth and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term (GDPC1 * UNRATE) does not significantly improve prediction accuracy over single-series models (F-test p > 0.05).
